string.text: The DP-27, also known as the Degtyaryov machine gun, is a Soviet light machine gun developed in the 1920s and widely used during World War II. Recognizable by its distinctive top-mounted pan magazine, the DP-27 fires the 7.62?54mmR cartridge and is known for its simplicity, reliability, and ruggedness in harsh battlefield conditions. Though it has a relatively low rate of fire compared to other machine guns of the era, its ease of maintenance and sturdy construction made it a dependable support weapon for Soviet infantry throughout the war.
Magazine for the DP-27 Machinegun, houses 47 rounds of 7.62x54r ammunition.
